headline: How The Five Below (FIVE) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Targets And Expectations
summary: The latest analyst update on Five Below lifts the fair value estimate from US$229.32 to US$261.32, implying a higher long term price target for the shares. That shift lines up with recent Street commentary, where bullish voices are moving targets into the mid to high US$200s, while more cautious analysts still question how much optimism is already priced in. As you read on, you will see how to interpret these changing targets and track the evolving story around growth, execution and...

headline: Bank of America’s Profit Increases 17%

headline: Morgan Stanley and Bank of American Wealth Units Bring in Billions of New Money
summary: Bank of America’s wealth unit, which includes a private bank and Merrill, reported asset flows of $20 billion for the quarter.  For the year-ago period, Morgan Stanley hauled in $93.8 billion.  Managing money on behalf of affluent investors has become a big business for banks, which prize the steady revenues wealth management can generate.

headline: Bank of America’s Stock Traders Book Record Revenue Amid Volatility
summary: Bank of America’s equities trading business drove record-high revenue in the first quarter as geopolitical tensions and artificial intelligence-related concerns prompted intense stock market volatility.  The second-largest U.S. bank said equities sales and trading revenue of $2.8 billion was a record, up nearly 30% from $2.2 billion a year earlier, on heightened client activity.  Bank of America said its overall sales and trading revenue jumped 13% from a year ago to $6.4 billion, its highest in more than a decade.

content.title: Bank of America’s Stock Traders Book Record Revenue Amid Volatility
content.summary: Bank of America’s equities trading business drove record-high revenue in the first quarter as geopolitical tensions and artificial intelligence-related concerns prompted intense stock market volatility.  The second-largest U.S. bank said equities sales and trading revenue of $2.8 billion was a record, up nearly 30% from $2.2 billion a year earlier, on heightened client activity.  Bank of America said its overall sales and trading revenue jumped 13% from a year ago to $6.4 billion, its highest in more than a decade.

headline: Like Other Bank CEOs, BofA's Moynihan Says U.S. Economy Is ‘Resilient’
summary: Bank of America Chief Executive Officer Brian Moynihan said the U.S. economy is “resilient,” joining a chorus of bank CEOs who see similar strength in the face of geopolitical tensions.  “We remain watchful of evolving risks,” Moynihan, who runs the second-largest U.S. lender, said in a statement.  “However, we saw healthy client activity, including solid consumer spending and stable asset quality, indicating a resilient American economy.”

content.title: Like Other Bank CEOs, BofA's Moynihan Says U.S. Economy Is ‘Resilient’
content.summary: Bank of America Chief Executive Officer Brian Moynihan said the U.S. economy is “resilient,” joining a chorus of bank CEOs who see similar strength in the face of geopolitical tensions.  “We remain watchful of evolving risks,” Moynihan, who runs the second-largest U.S. lender, said in a statement.  “However, we saw healthy client activity, including solid consumer spending and stable asset quality, indicating a resilient American economy.”

headline: Bank of America’s (NYSE:BAC) Q1 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates
summary: Financial services giant Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 6.7% year on year to $30.27 billion. Its GAAP profit of $1.11 per share was 9.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Hedge Funds Pivot to Bearish Dollar Bets on US-Iran Talks Optimism
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Hedge funds are increasingly downbeat on the dollar as the prospect of renewed US-Iran talks and a possible peace deal sap the currency’s war-driven strength. Investors added to their bearish dollar trades this month through April 10, based on a proprietary trading model from Morgan Stanley.In the options market, so-called risk reversals on the Bloomberg dollar index show the premium to hedge against a stronger dollar compared with bets against a weaker greenback has narrowed this

headline: BofA's trading desk avoided a single daily loss in Q1 amid market turmoil
summary: The market volatility of the last three months, caused partly by the war in Iran, has been a boon to Wall Street, as another set of banks reported strong profits helped by wild swings in markets and companies looking to make deals while the economy remains resilient.  Bank of America said on Wednesday that first-quarter stock trading revenues were $2.8 billion, a 30% jump from last year, while Morgan Stanley's much larger trading desk saw its equity trading revenues rise 25% to $5.15 billion.  Morgan Stanley also had a very strong performance on its bond-trading desk, posting a 29% jump in revenues to $3.36 billion.

content.title: BofA's trading desk avoided a single daily loss in Q1 amid market turmoil
content.summary: The market volatility of the last three months, caused partly by the war in Iran, has been a boon to Wall Street, as another set of banks reported strong profits helped by wild swings in markets and companies looking to make deals while the economy remains resilient.  Bank of America said on Wednesday that first-quarter stock trading revenues were $2.8 billion, a 30% jump from last year, while Morgan Stanley's much larger trading desk saw its equity trading revenues rise 25% to $5.15 billion.  Morgan Stanley also had a very strong performance on its bond-trading desk, posting a 29% jump in revenues to $3.36 billion.

headline: Shares of Morgan Stanley and BofA Rise on Earnings Beats
summary: Morgan Stanley and Bank of America’s stocks were up 5.3% and 1%, respectively, on Wednesday morning after the companies reported better-than-expected first-quarter earnings.  The KBW Nasdaq Bank Index was up 0.5% and the S&P 500 was up 0.3%.  Other bank stocks were also up Wednesday, with a few exceptions.

headline: S&P 500 Hits Record High on Hopes for Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The S&P 500 continued its rally after rising to an all-time high on Wednesday, as a potential peace deal in the Middle East and a strong start to corporate earnings season spurred investor optimism. The benchmark index was up 0.7%, putting it above a late-January record. Bank of America Corp. and Morgan Stanley rose as their equity traders posted strong revenue beats. The tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 was up 1%.The US and Iran are considering extending their ceasefire that ends on Tuesday

headline: Five Things We Learned From Bank Earnings
summary: The nation’s biggest banks this week painted a reassuring picture of the U.S. economy, saying consumers are still spending despite surging gas prices since the Iran war began.  Trading desks are raking in money from volatile markets and dealmakers are busier than they have been in a while.

headline: US Stocks Close at Record Highs on Peace Hopes: Markets Wrap
summary: (Bloomberg) -- US stocks closed at record highs on Wednesday, as a potential peace deal in the Middle East and a strong start to corporate earnings season stoked investor optimism. The S&P 500 finished the day up 0.8%, extending a dramatic two-week surge from its late March lows. The tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 was up 1.4%, also a record. Bank of America Corp. and Morgan Stanley rose as their equity traders posted strong revenue beats. Hopes that peace efforts between the US and Iran will succeed have
